verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 (Q1ZYL8) is annotated as nuclear by NCBI/Alliance of Genome Resources and lacks a predicted TM domain or signal peptide, making it structurally divergent from surface-displaying IZUMO family members (IZUMO1–3 are sperm TM proteins). Pre-no checklist: no cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs documented; no TM precursor/shedding architecture; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering; no documented PM minority pool or secretory cycling; no gene-specific evidence of cancer/cell-death/activation-induced outer-leaflet display or germline-restricted surface pool; not a lysosomal TM protein. HGNC family lineage (IZUMO) creates a mild surface prior, but IZUMO4 is the family outlier without membrane-anchoring topology, and per-gene nuclear localization evidence overrides class analogy.
predicted_key_uncertainty: IZUMO4 is poorly characterized; the nuclear call rests on a brief Alliance annotation and absence of TM domain — a minority PM pool or germline-surface context cannot be fully excluded without direct experimental data.
